title: The Eternal Skald
description:
v0.10.24 — Sharper map vision. The Skald now reads detailed fantasy maps far more thoroughly. Map captures are sent as lossless PNG at up to 4096px (no more JPEG blur on small labels), and a rewritten cartographer prompt explicitly directs the model to transcribe text labels/place names, spot small symbols and icons, trace faint paths and roads, and catalogue every settlement and structure — each with a confidence level. A new grid-sectioning analysis (the Map Analysis Quality setting: Fast, Balanced or Thorough) splits large maps into a 2×2 or 3×3 grid, runs a zoomed detail pass over each section plus a full-map overview, and intelligently combines the findings — dramatically improving recall of small text and out-of-the-way landmarks. Three new settings (Map Analysis Quality, Max Map Resolution, Map Image Format) tune the cost/quality trade-off, and the Skald now recommends strong vision models (gpt-4o, claude-3-5-sonnet, gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-2.5-pro) and whispers a GM advisory when a lightweight model (e.g. gpt-4o-mini) is likely to miss fine detail. Purely additive, GM-only, still read-only on the base map, and degrades gracefully.

v0.10.23 — Map vision (the Skald can SEE your map). The Skald now looks at the actual background artwork of your scene with a vision-capable AI model and scouts it: it identifies terrain, landmarks, paths, hazards and points of interest, posts a saga-styled Scouting card, and auto-scribes each location into the Living Chronicle (linked to the scene). New scenes are scouted automatically once (cached on the scene so it never re-analyses needlessly), and you can force a fresh scout at any time with the new GM-only !scout command (aliases !survey, !analyze-map). The base map image is downscaled and sent as JPEG for token efficiency; only the base map is read (never tokens, drawings or fog). Two new settings — Auto-Analyze Scenes (default on) and Vision Model (inherit your narration model, or pin a known vision model) — control it. If the chosen model can't see images, the Skald skips analysis with a quiet GM notice rather than wasting a call. Purely additive, GM-only, read-only on the map, and degrades gracefully.

v0.10.22 — Map & scene awareness. The Skald can now see your active map. A new read-only scene reader gathers the current scene name, its marked locations (journal-pin notes, resolved to the linked journal entry) and its notable visible tokens (hidden GM-only tokens are excluded), and folds a concise CURRENT SCENE / Visible Locations / Notable Tokens block into the AI context. The Skald is told it can read the map and may reference those real places by name — especially when suggesting a destination for a journey or vow — but never forces it and never invents pins that are not on the map. Purely additive, read-only (no scene manipulation), token-efficient, and degrades gracefully when no scene is active.

An AI-powered storyteller, oracle interpreter, and tactical enemy controller for Ironsworn and Ironsworn: Delve campaigns in Foundry VTT v14. Powered by Abacus AI ChatLLM. Just type ! followed by your words to speak with the Skald, or !skald-help for all commands.

v0.10.21 — Reach a Milestone no longer double-marks. Triggering Reach a Milestone (by clicking its inline link in the narration, or via the move list) now advances your active vow by its rank exactly once. A bug caused the vow to be marked twice — once when the move was triggered and again during the follow-up narration — so the track jumped by 2× rank (or appeared inconsistent). The narration step is now told the mechanics were already applied, so it narrates the single, correct result. The milestone also recognises both Ironsworn and Starforged reach_a_milestone moves and works on vows you swore by hand on the sheet. Added an end-to-end regression test for the full triggerMove → mark-progress chain.

v0.10.19 — Bulletproofed the AI-prompt templates. Removed the last \` escaped-backtick characters from the foe-prompt template literals (the word unique is now written with plain quotes), so the script can never be misread as having an unterminated template. Verified clean in five independent parsers — Node/V8 in both script and module mode, Acorn, Babel, and a real Chrome ES-module load. If you still see a "missing ) after argument list" error at line 1342, it is a CACHED copy of an old script, not the current code: fully update the module, then hard-refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) or restart Foundry. No functional change.

v0.10.18 — Republished to clear stale caches. If the module failed to load with a JavaScript syntax error (e.g. "missing ) after argument list" around line 1342) even after updating, your browser or Foundry was still running a cached copy of the old script. This release republishes the verified-clean script (validated in strict ES-module mode) and bumps the version so Foundry re-downloads it. No functional change from v0.10.17 — if you still see the error, do a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) or restart Foundry.

v0.10.17 — Critical hotfix. Fixes a JavaScript "missing ) after argument list" syntax error that prevented the module from loading at all. Stray un-escaped backticks inside an AI-prompt template literal (introduced with the v0.10.14 foe catalogue) prematurely terminated the string when Foundry loaded the script as an ES module. All scripts now validate in strict module mode.

v0.10.16 — Clean slate for a new campaign. A new GM-only !skald-reset command (alias !skald-wipe) wipes the chronicle so you can start a fresh saga: after a confirmation dialog it deletes all unlocked Skald-scribed journal entries, clears the semantic memory (RAG) vectors, resets the conversation history, and empties the campaign timeline — then whispers a report of exactly what was cleared. Your own (non-Skald) journals are never touched, and any entry you lock (its the-eternal-skald.locked flag set to true) is preserved.

v0.10.15 — Journeys that just work. Rolling Undertake a Journey now automatically opens a journey progress track (named for your destination) when none is open, and marks progress on a hit — so Reach Your Destination always has a track to roll against instead of failing with «No open journey track…». The Skald can also advance or complete a specific vow/journey by its exact title from the fiction — a new [[EFFECT: mark_progress "Your Vow Title"]] directive marks the named track, and narrative completion no longer requires a roll first. Your open vows and journeys are now listed by title in the AI's live game state, so the Skald references the right track every time.

v0.10.14 — Real foes from the official compendia. The Skald now picks regular foes from the official foundry-ironsworn foe compendia (Ironsworn Foes + Delve Foes) instead of inventing names. The full catalogue of available foes (grouped by rank) is embedded in the AI prompt, and the Skald is instructed to draw routine encounters from that list verbatim so ranks come straight from the rulebook. Important narrative foes — a named boss or unique antagonist the story is built around — may still be custom-created; the Skald marks them with a unique keyword so they are sized intentionally. A GM-only advisory quietly flags when a routine foe isn't an official compendium foe. The foe list is loaded and cached on world load.

v0.10.13 — Vow tracking reads straight from your sheet. Fixes a bug where the Skald's vow card could show a phantom vow (e.g. "Vow", rank 1, 0/10 boxes, marked complete) that had nothing to do with the real vow on your character sheet. The Skald now resolves and displays vows/journeys/tracks directly from the foundry-ironsworn items on your sheet, read fresh every time — progress comes from system.current (ticks ÷ 4 = boxes) and completion from system.completed, so the card always mirrors the sheet. Clicking the bare word "vow" now opens your actual current vow (e.g. "The Truth of the Star-Fall") instead of a generic phantom, and marking progress writes back to the sheet immediately. Generic nouns like "vow"/"journey" are no longer turned into misleading phantom links.

v0.10.12 — Works on hosted Foundry (direct browser→AI fallback). Fixes the runtime /skald-api/chat 404 (Not Found) error that stopped the Skald from reaching the AI on hosted/managed Foundry (e.g. Foundry VTT on Abacus), where you can't launch Foundry with the server-side --import flag the server hook needs. The Skald now automatically falls back to calling the AI directly from your browser when the server hook isn't loaded, so it just works out of the box. A new Connection Mode setting (Auto — default, Server hook only, or Direct browser→AI) lets you control this. The default Abacus AI endpoint allows the cross-origin request, so no setup is required.

v0.10.11 — Vow & journey completion fix. Fulfilling a vow or reaching a destination now closes the correct track. Previously, completing a quest after a Fulfill Your Vow / Reach Your Destination roll could fail with «Track "Reach Your Destination" not found», because the system looked for a track named after the move instead of your real, player-named vow/journey. The Skald now remembers which track a progress move actually rolled against and closes that one — and if the name is uncertain or omitted, it falls back to the most recent open vow/journey of the right kind.

v0.10.10 — Move suggestions woven into the prose. Suggested moves are no longer shown as separate A Move Beckons / What Comes Next cards. Instead the Skald names the fitting move inside its narration, where it appears as a subtle clickable link woven into the story — both in ordinary narration (!skald, !scene, !combat) and in the post-roll outcome narration after a move resolves. Clicking the link rolls the move through the same progress-aware path as before (so progress moves like Reach Your Destination / Fulfill Your Vow roll against their track). No more standalone suggestion bubbles cluttering the chat.

v0.10.9 — Faithful progress-track integration. After studying the live foundry-ironsworn source, the Skald now creates vows, journeys, bonds and combat foes exactly as the system itself does: a single Item type progress distinguished by system.subtype (vow / progress / bond / foe), with proper numeric challenge ranks and tick-based progress. A stray, non-schema notes field was removed, the standard sort order is applied, and combat foes now use the system's real foe subtype. The Skald also recognises tracks you create by hand on the sheet — sworn vows, journeys and foes — so progress, completion and the Fulfill Your Vow / Reach Your Destination rolls work on them too.

v0.10.8 — Inline move suggestions return. Move suggestions are once again woven as clickable links inside the narration (reverting the separate A Move Beckons / What Comes Next button-cards). Clicking an inline move link now routes through the progress-aware trigger, so progress moves like Reach Your Destination roll correctly instead of failing with "no dialog and no rollable stat". Journey-track lookup gained a fallback that finds legacy or hand-made journey tracks (older progress-subtype tracks without the journey flag). Finally, vow / journey creation now surfaces failures — if there is no active character or the data is rejected, the Skald whispers the GM exactly why instead of failing silently, and track ranks are written as proper numeric challenge ranks.

v0.10.4 — Manual-roll narration fix. When you roll a move from the Skald's What Comes Next buttons (or any manual fallback roll), the Skald now reliably detects the result and continues the story. Previously these rolls — posted as the module's own manual-roll cards — were skipped by the chat hook and never narrated, breaking the narrative flow.

v0.10.3 — Combat track cleanup. Ironsworn is fought one foe at a time, so when a new fight begins the Skald now automatically closes any combat track left open from a previous fight — no more orphaned, untracked foe tracks lingering on your sheet. Several foes introduced in the same scene still coexist as separate tracks, and a new Auto-Close Old Combat Tracks world setting (default on) lets you opt out for simultaneous fights.

v0.10.2 — Journey completion. Journeys are now first-class progress tracks just like vows: the Skald can begin one with create_journey and close it with complete_journey, and progress-track cards show a manual Reach Destination (mark complete) button for journeys (works even with AI-applied effects disabled). Journeys are now identified by their journey subtype and journey-flavoured throughout the UI.

v0.10.1 — Vow completion fix. Vows (and other progress tracks) can now actually be marked complete. A new complete_vow AI effect (plus synonyms) lets the Skald close out a fulfilled vow, and a manual Mark Complete / Fulfill Vow button on progress-track cards lets you fulfil a vow yourself at any progress level (works even with AI-applied effects disabled). Also fixes vow identification to match the modern foundry-ironsworn data model.

v0.10.0 — Follow-up move suggestions. After narrating a resolved move, the Skald now offers a What Comes Next card with two one-click follow-up moves to roll next (plus Roll Any Other Move), mirroring the pre-roll suggestion card. The Skald may no longer invent moves — every suggested move is validated against the real Ironsworn / Starforged move catalogue.

v0.9.3 — Abacus AI endpoint fix. Corrected the Abacus AI provider/default endpoint to the working https://routellm.abacus.ai/v1/chat/completions (the v0.9.2 default, api.abacus.ai/v0, was non-functional). Existing worlds still pinned to the bad URL are auto-migrated on load — no manual change needed. Backwards-compatible; you still supply your own API key and model name.

v0.9.2 — Abacus AI by default. The AI Provider dropdown now includes Abacus AI (the engine that powers the Skald) as the recommended, default option, pointing at the Abacus AI chat-completions endpoint out of the box. The full provider order is now Abacus AI (default), OpenAI, OpenRouter, Google AI (Gemini) and Custom. Additive and backwards-compatible — you still supply your own API key and model name.

v0.9.1 — multi-provider made easy. A new AI Provider dropdown in Module Settings lets you switch between providers in one click — picking a provider auto-fills the API Endpoint with its OpenAI-compatible URL (you still supply your own API key and model name). Backwards-compatible and additive.

v0.9.0 — the Attentive Chronicle. The Skald now reads the room: context-aware suggestions weave subtle, situation-aware next-step hints from the active scene, location and recent story threads into its narration (never forcing the story). Lore contradiction alerts quietly compare each freshly recorded fact against established chronicle lore and whisper a GM-only advisory when the saga seems to trip over itself — nothing is ever changed automatically. Idle auto-summaries let a lull in play weave a Session Chronicle on its own, so a recap is never lost when !end-session is forgotten. Customizable link styles let you recolor and re-icon in-chat entity links to taste via the new !link-style command. Under the hood, entity indexing is optimized with a generation-keyed journal cache, so linking stays snappy across campaigns of 100+ journals. All additive, backwards-compatible, and degrade gracefully. Still an early (v0.x) alpha — expect rough edges.

v0.8.0 — the Living Chronicle, deepened. The chronicle now maps relationships between the folk and places it records: the Skald reads the bonds in its narration, links them bidirectionally as journal content-links, and shows a Connections section inside each entry — review the whole web with the new !relationships (alias !map) command. A persistent Timeline remembers every beat of the saga across sessions and reloads; browse or filter it with !timeline. NPCs, locations and discoveries gain structured template fields (rank, harm, goals, region, dangers, resources and more) that the AI fills in and you can author by hand with the new !template dialog. Finally, smarter entity detection recognises aliases and name variations ("the captain" → Captain Reeves) so the chronicle stops creating duplicates and links every mention. Purely additive, backwards-compatible with existing journals, and degrades gracefully.

v0.7.0 — Entity linking expansion. The Skald now links even more of what it speaks, directly in chat. Oracle names become one-click links that roll that very oracle (e.g. Action Oracle), Progress Tracks from your active character become links that open a status card with a one-click Mark Progress (by rank) button, and Assets become links that open the asset's own sheet (with a graceful chat-card fallback). Asset matching uses fuzzy lookup across your compendia, and the entity index now refreshes automatically as items, actors and tokens change — so links stay current mid-session. Builds on the v0.6.0 clickable NPCs, locations and moves; purely additive and degrades gracefully.

v0.6.0 — Clickable entities in narration. Names the Skald speaks are now linked inline in chat: chronicled NPCs, locations and discoveries become Foundry content links that open their Journal Entry, and known Ironsworn moves become one-click links that open the foundry-ironsworn system's own official move dialog directly (resolved by the move's Datasworn ID). A new world setting Link Entities in Narration (default on) toggles the feature; it is purely additive and degrades gracefully.

v0.5.0 — AI Memory. The Skald now remembers your whole saga. Every chronicle entry is embedded into a private, in-browser semantic memory (Browser-Based RAG) and the most relevant NPCs, locations, facts and threads are recalled automatically before the Skald speaks — so continuity holds across sessions without bloating the prompt. It runs entirely in your browser (no server, no cloud, no API keys for memory); a small model downloads once and is cached. !remind is now semantic, and two new commands — !reindex (rebuild memory) and !rag-status (inspect it) — join six new settings. Memory degrades gracefully: the AI works fully even when it can't load.

v0.4.0 — the Living Chronicle. The Skald now automatically scribes your saga into Foundry Journal Entries as it unfolds: NPCs, locations and discoveries each get their own entry, while world facts and story threads accumulate in rolling journals — all neatly organized into folders under The Eternal Skald. Journaling runs quietly in the background and never interrupts narration. New commands let you review your world: !journals lists what's been recorded, !mysteries surfaces open threads, !remind recalls what the chronicle knows about a topic, and !end-session weaves a saga-styled session recap. Four new settings control auto-journaling, notification verbosity, player visibility, and session summaries. Builds on the v0.3.3 real-time streaming, the v0.3.2 bare ! alias and AI Mode toggle, v0.3.1 compendium enemy ranks, and the v0.3.0 automatic combat system, on top of deep integration with the foundry-ironsworn rules engine. Works standalone too — Ironsworn features activate automatically when the system is present.

recommends.reason: Optional but recommended. When the Ironsworn system is active, the Skald integrates with its rules engine to trigger moves, read character state, and narrate official roll outcomes. The module also works standalone in any system, but Ironsworn-specific mechanics are then unavailable.
